channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Iran warns Europe of "act of war" if military involvement
item.description: Iranian officials issued stern warnings to European nations on March 5, 2026, stating that any military involvement in the escalating conflict would be considered an act of war and would trigger Iranian retaliation against European targets.

item.title: CENTCOM reports Natanz 90%+ destroyed
item.description: US Central Command released assessment on March 4, 2026 confirming that the Natanz nuclear enrichment facility has sustained damage affecting over 90% of its operational capacity. Satellite imagery and intelligence reports indicate extensive structural and equipment damage across the primary uranium enrichment complex.

item.title: UN Emergency Session convenes
item.description: The United Nations Security Council held an emergency session on March 3, 2026 to address the escalating crisis. Diplomatic representatives from permanent member states convened to discuss de-escalation measures, humanitarian concerns, and potential international intervention protocols as regional tensions reached critical levels.

item.title: Iran retaliates with 500+ missiles, 2000+ drones
item.description: Iran launched a massive retaliatory strike on March 2, 2026 in response to initial coalition military operations. The attack involved over 500 ballistic and cruise missiles combined with more than 2,000 unmanned aerial vehicles targeting coalition positions across the region. Air defense systems were activated at multiple locations to intercept incoming ordnance.

item.title: CENTCOM announces Khamenei killed in initial strikes
item.description: US Central Command announced on March 1, 2026 that Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was killed in the initial coordinated strikes launched by coalition forces against Iran. The announcement marked a significant development with potential implications for Iran's political and military leadership structure.
